step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

We are looking for which drug does NOT cause gingival hyperplasia. All listed drugs—carbamazepine, verapamil, and sodium valproate—are known to induce gingival hyperplasia as side effects due to their pharmacological properties (e.g., anticonvulsant or calcium channel blocker actions). However, bleomycin is an anticancer agent used primarily for its cytotoxic effect on DNA; it has no established link with gingival hyperplasia. Therefore, among these choices, only bleomycin is not associated with this adverse effect.
raw_response:
Answer:

We are looking for which drug does NOT cause gingival hyperplasia. All listed drugs—carbamazepine, verapamil, and sodium valproate—are known to induce gingival hyperplasia as side effects due to their pharmacological properties (e.g., anticonvulsant or calcium channel blocker actions). However, bleomycin is an anticancer agent used primarily for its cytotoxic effect on DNA; it has no established link with gingival hyperplasia. Therefore, among these choices, only bleomycin is not associated with this adverse effect.

Answer: D: Bleomycin
